site.title: Anastasia Yuki Mach
site.description: Personal portfolio of Anastasia Yuki Mach, a student exploring software and hardware engineering.

profile.name: Anastasia Yuki Mach
profile.bio: Sup guys, welcome to my portfolio
profile.about: Hey, I'm Anastasia — a Japanese-American student who got curious about how things work, both in code and in circuits, and never really stopped. I split my time between software (mostly Python, C++, and JavaScript) and hardware (microcontrollers, sensors, and whatever I can solder together), and I like projects that make me learn both at once. This site is one of those projects — built from scratch as a way to practice, document what I'm learning, and give my work an actual home. Still very much a work in progress, just like me.

highlights.title: Built This Portfolio
highlights.description: Designed and coded this entire site from scratch — layout, content system, and all — as a hands-on way to practice front-end development.

highlights.title: Started My Engineering Journey
highlights.description: Began learning software and hardware engineering side by side, treating every small project as a chance to pick up something new.

projects.title: Personal Portfolio Website
projects.description: This very site — a JSON-driven, multi-page portfolio built with plain HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and deployed on GitHub Pages.

projects.title: Hardware Tinkering Log
projects.description: An ongoing collection of small electronics experiments — microcontrollers, sensors, and circuits — as I learn hardware engineering hands-on.

projects.title: Code Practice Playground
projects.description: Small scripts and exercises exploring different languages and problem-solving approaches while building core programming skills.

blog.title: Welcome To My Blog
blog.excerpt: Kicking off my dev and hardware learning log — here's what to expect.

experience.title: Independent Learner
experience.description: Studying software and hardware engineering fundamentals through online courses, personal projects, and hands-on experimentation.

education.title: Software & Hardware Engineering Fundamentals
education.description: Building a foundation across programming, computer systems, and embedded electronics.
